mySecurity.title: Моя безопасность
mySecurity.passkeyNotSecureContext: Эта страница не является безопасным контекстом. Используйте HTTPS.
mySecurity.passkeyDeleteConfirm: Удалить этот passkey? Если это единственный метод входа без пароля, вы можете потерять доступ.

adminSettings.title: Настройки
adminSettings.smtpPasswordHint: Пароль не отображается после сохранения. Оставьте пустым, чтобы не менять.
adminSettings.emailVerificationRequireLoginHint: Пользователи без подтверждённого email не смогут войти
adminSettings.emailVerificationRequireLoginWarning: Существующие пользователи без подтверждённого email могут потерять доступ. Сначала завершите миграцию.
adminSettings.passkeyDiscoverableLoginHint: Allow the login page to use registered discoverable passkeys.
adminSettings.passkeyHttpsHint: Passkeys require an HTTPS origin. RP ID is usually the current domain; allowed origins should include full https:// URLs.
adminSettings.authDisablePasswordWarning: Before disabling password login, make sure at least one admin OAuth or Passkey login path is available.
adminSettings.registerHint: When enabled, the login page shows a registration link. If the default status is disabled, new users must be approved by an administrator.
adminSettings.serverSecurityHint: This setting is applied through the hbbs runtime command must-login/ml. After container restart, it falls back to the MUST_LOGIN environment default. When enabled, RustDesk client connections require a login token.
adminSettings.serverSecuritySaveSuccess: Server security settings applied to the current runtime
adminSettings.serverSecurityApplyFailed: Server returned a state different from the requested value. Refresh and try again.
adminSettings.serverSecurityClientHint: The official RustDesk client does not currently provide a dedicated “login required” connection error. Unauthenticated connections may show Broken pipe, LOGIN_REQUIRED, Key mismatch, or a generic connection failure. Sign in to the client first and confirm that API and hbbs share the same RUSTDESK_API_JWT_KEY.
adminSettings.authOverviewSmtpRequiredWarning: Registration email verification is enabled, but SMTP is disabled or incomplete. Users will not be able to receive registration codes.
adminSettings.authOverviewPasswordLockoutWarning: Password login is disabled, but discoverable passkey login is not enabled. Confirm that another administrator login method is available to avoid lockout.
adminSettings.authOverviewSmtpAnyRequiredWarning: Email-code features are enabled, but SMTP is disabled or incomplete. Registration codes, email-change verification, or login verification may not work.
adminSettings.authOverviewSmtpTestHint: A complete configuration does not guarantee delivery. Use the SMTP tab to send a test email.
adminSettings.smtpReadyHint: SMTP configuration is complete enough for email-code features. Send a test email before enabling verification flows.
adminSettings.smtpRequiredHint: Email-code features require SMTP to be enabled with host, port, and from email configured.
adminSettings.smtpPasswordRequiredHint: A username is set but no SMTP password is saved. Save a password or clear the username.
adminSettings.smtpTestRequiredHint: Saving SMTP only validates configuration shape. Send a test email to verify real delivery.
adminSettings.smtpLastTestSuccess: Last SMTP test succeeded in this browser session.
adminSettings.smtpLastTestFailed: Last SMTP test failed. Check the message and server logs, then update the settings.
adminSettings.emailVerificationSmtpRequiredHint: These options send email codes. Configure SMTP and send a test email before saving requirements.
adminSettings.emailVerificationSmtpTestHint: SMTP appears configured. Send a test email on the SMTP tab before enabling requirements for production users.
adminSettings.smtpTestUsesSavedSettingsHint: SMTP test sends with the saved server-side settings. Save changes before testing.
adminSettings.mustLoginVerifiedBehavior: Verified: when must-login is enabled, an unauthenticated client is rejected with LOGIN_REQUIRED, while an authenticated client can connect successfully. Different client versions may still show a generic connection failure; use current test evidence and server logs as the source of truth.
adminSettings.mustLoginReadinessIntro: Confirm the client login path in this order before applying “Require client login” to the current runtime.
adminSettings.mustLoginReadinessClientConfig: Copy the client import configuration from Profile / Server Connection Credentials and confirm API Server, ID Server, Relay Server, and Key are correct.
adminSettings.mustLoginReadinessWebauth: The RustDesk client account page should show the webauth login option. Clicking it opens the Web Admin authorization page in a browser; after sign-in and confirmation, the client should receive a logged-in state.
adminSettings.mustLoginReadinessConnection: First verify that an authenticated client can connect, then verify that an unauthenticated client is rejected, so production enablement is not confused with Key or network issues.
adminSettings.mustLoginUnauthedExpected: Unauthenticated client: connection should be rejected; the currently verified path returns LOGIN_REQUIRED.
adminSettings.mustLoginAuthedExpected: Authenticated client: connection with a valid token should succeed.
